# Nick Drake · Pink Moon — Living Tribute

> A fan-made, non-commercial, interactive tribute page to English singer-songwriter
> Nick Drake (born 19 June 1948, died 25 November 1974), built around the artwork
> of his third and final studio album, *Pink Moon*, released on Island Records on
> 25 February 1972.

## What this site is

The original Pink Moon cover art (painted by Michael Trevithick) was separated
into individual layers — the pink moon/sphere, a teacup, a leaf, a ghost-like
face, a postage stamp, a shell — and recomposed in the browser with Three.js
(WebGL). Each element floats and drifts on its own, responds to mouse/touch
parallax, and can be dragged and repositioned by visitors. The full album can
be played inline via embedded YouTube videos, track by track or in sequence.

## Who Nick Drake was

Nick Drake was an English singer-songwriter and musician known for his
intricate guitar playing, deep, melancholic voice, and short discography of
three studio albums: *Five Leaves Left* (1969), *Bryter Layter* (1970), and
*Pink Moon* (1972). He received little commercial recognition during his
lifetime and died in 1974 at age 26. His music was rediscovered by a much
wider audience in the decades since, and *Pink Moon* — recorded in two
late-night sessions with just voice and guitar (plus one piano overdub) — is
now widely regarded as one of the most intimate and influential folk albums
ever made.

## Pink Moon (1972) tracklist

1. Pink Moon
2. Place to Be
3. Road
4. Which Will
5. Horn
6. Things Behind the Sun
7. Know
8. Parasite
9. Free Ride
10. Harvest Breed
11. From the Morning

## Purpose

This page exists purely to celebrate and spread awareness of Nick Drake's
music and art, for fans and newcomers alike. It is not affiliated with
Island Records, the Drake estate, or any rights holder. All music is
streamed via embedded YouTube players; no audio is hosted on this site.
Album artwork is used respectfully, in a transformed/animated form, as a
tribute.

## Site

- Homepage: https://fabianimv.github.io/nick-drake-tribute/
- Source: https://github.com/FabianIMV/nick-drake-tribute
